g.text: Netty Private Protocol Round-Trip: NettyClient <-> NettyServer (login + heartbeat)
for each entry: writeInt(keyLen) + writeBytes(UTF-8 key)
attachment empty -> writeInt(0); body null -> writeInt(0)
log "Client receive server heart beat message"
ScheduledFuture is cancelled so the 5s task stops.
- On LOGIN_RESP(0) -> HeartBeatReqHandler schedules a new 5s task
- Node-check dedup on server uses remoteAddress string; a reconnected
socket from the same host:port reuses the entry if previously cleared
- ReadTimeoutException after 50s of silence on the server side
or marshalling failure / crcCode mismatch reading)
ctx.close(); channel tears down, triggers reconnect in finally
body == null -> [0:int] 4-byte zero sentinel, NOT a marshalled 0
length field is backpatched via sendBuf.setInt(4, readableBytes())
to cover crcCode + length + sessionId + type + priority + attachments + body.
Heartbeat is one-way request with response; if the server's
HeartBeatRespHandler is removed or mis-wired the client still logs
"send heart beat" every 5s but never sees HEARTBEAT_RESP, so
